evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot clearly shows NS/up/down classification with threshold lines and top-5 gene labels; scatter density communicates the ~3000-gene dataset well.
evidence.typography: pdffonts reports Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans fonts — not Type 42/TrueType selectable text; script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or uses a non-default font family.
evidence.palette: Red/blue for directional up/down is defensible; grey for NS is fine. However red-green is not used (good), but the palette is not explicitly verified colorblind-safe (deuteranopia: red-grey distinction may be marginal); no deliberate colorblind palette (e.g. Okabe-Ito) selected.
evidence.layout_density: Figure fills 6×5 in canvas with tight_layout; no excessive whitespace, legend placed upper-left away from data, axis spines trimmed; y-range auto-fit to data.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes the figure and matches it; legend counts are embedded. Minor issue: gene labels are indices (G<idx>) not gene names, which the caption acknowledges but Reviewer 2 might still flag as unsatisfying.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is produced but pdffonts shows Type 3 (non-selectable, raster-embedded) fonts — not true editable vector text; script does not set matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states thresholds (|log2FC|>=1, -log10(p)>=2), describes color encoding, mentions dashed threshold lines, and notes top-5 labels — specific and informative.

overall_strengths: Clear, well-labeled volcano plot that correctly communicates the three-group classification with threshold lines and top-hit annotations.
All required output files are present; caption is precise with numerical threshold values and accurately describes what is shown.
overall_weaknesses: PDF fonts are Type 3 (bitmapped) rather than Type 42 (TrueType/selectable); the script never sets pdf.fonttype=42, making text non-selectable and non-editable in the vector output.
Default DejaVu Sans font family is used with no explicit colorblind-safe palette (e.g., Okabe-Ito); red/blue on grey is acceptable but not rigorously validated for all color-vision deficiencies.
